Let's discover Docusaurus in less than 5 minutes.
An online mathematical encyclopedia devoted to Deriving and exploring mathematics beyond the classroom for high school students. All Things Are Derivable

Our mission is to make mathematical reasoning transparent, tangible, and telling through Derivation while offering additional pathways for inquisitive and motivated students to explore mathematics with greter rigor and depth.
This project aims to demystify mathematical Deriving and to foster deeper curiosity through clear and careful reasoning.

Throughout this project, Derivation is treated as a proper noun, referring to the process of obtain a result through meticulous, step-by-step calculation. As the main theme of Art of Derivation, all words related to Derivation (such as Derive, Deriving) are highlighted in the main theme color. Some closely related terms, though not strictly equivalent, including Proof, Justification, and Logical Argument, all of which are integral to the idea of Derivation presented in this project. My understanding of Derivation was mainly developed from my AP Physics C: Mechanics course, where the Free Response Questions frequently require student to Derive expressions from fundamental definitions or equations in the provided equations sheet.
This project is built on Docusaurus and deployed on GitHub Pages. Below are detailed format information.

All documents are stored as Markdown files and use either the .md or .mdx extension. All document file names are capitalized and connected using hyphens (-). All documents are stored under the docs directory, organized by sidebars.js. All referenced images (such as .jpg, .png, .svg) are stored in the static/img directory.
All documents start with the front matter to ensure the consistent format and navigation. More additional details, refer to Markdown Features | Docusaurus.
All mathematical expressions are rendered using KaTeX with remark-math and follow standard LaTeX\LaTeXLATE‚ÄčX syntax.

All Derivation processes are placed in collapsible blocks, implemented using <details>+<summary>. All Derivation processes are organized using the align* enviornment. All Derivation processes are completed with \blacksquare (‚Ė†\blacksquare‚Ė†), indicating Q.E.D. (quod erat demonstrandum), meaning "that which was to be demonstrated".

Note The Gray Admonition provides extra and unnecessary notes and information. Definition The Green Admonition provides definition about the given topic. Property, Identity, Solution... The Blue Admonition provides Properties, Identities, Solutions, and so on that is NOT designed to show the collapsable Derivation process. Derivation The collapsable Admonition provides detailed process for Derivation Question The Orange Admonition highlights the question for examples. Warning The Red Admonition provides information that is important to know.

The theme color of this project is Indigo (#4b0082) in light mode and Magenta (#ff61ff) in dark mode. The theme color is exclusively reserved for Derivation and its related concepts, rather than decorative styling.
The idea of the favicon came from ‚ąĶ\because‚ąĶ ($\because$) and ‚ąī\therefore‚ąī ($\therefore$).

All documents are organized in a logical sequence; following the given order is strongly recommended. Before viewing the full derivation through the collapsable bar, attempt to work through each step independently using pen and paper. Detailed solutions are provided in the collapsible sections for verification and deeper understanding. Maintaining a dedicated notebook for your own derivations is highly encouraged.

This page will explain Integral in a nut shell,

Below are 147 advanced integral formulas listed in the appendix of the Higher Mathematics (Tongji University).Each formula is Derived step by step. It may seem futile and impractival to memorize all the formulas, but it is a great opportunity to consolidate integration techniques and appreciate heuristics behind them.

In my AP Physics C Mechanics class, many students were struggling to Derive the terminal velocity of a free-falling object, and the primary reason was that they can't solve the Integral Formula #21. My teacher back then found a really sophisticated way through hyperbolic trigonometry substitution, and everyone was stunned by the complexity, but I came up with a simple algebraic transformation. Substitution is the most common integral technique in elementary calculus. Recall what we mentioned in common integrals that we use uuu and du\mathrm{d}udu instead of xxx and dx\mathrm{d}xdx, and this is because uuu may represent an entire function, not just a single variable. We will tackle with this more specifically in this section.

We will start with this integral question: ‚ąę3x2sin‚Ā°x3 dx= ?\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\ ?‚ąę3x2sinx3 dx= ? Note that there is an product, which is beyond all common integrals, so we need to simplify to an easier form. An example is below: Solution Let: u=x3u=x^3u=x3 dudx=3x2du=3x2 dx\begin{align*} \frac{\mathrm{d}u}{\mathrm{d}x}&=3x^2\\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}&=\color{red}{3x^2\ \mathrm{d}x}\\ \end{align*}dxdu‚Äčdu‚Äč=3x2=3x2 dx‚Äč Plug in back to the question: ‚ąę3x2sin‚Ā°x3 dx=‚ąęsin‚Ā°u du=‚ąęsin‚Ā°u du=‚ąícos‚Ā°u+C=‚ąícos‚Ā°x3+C\begin{align*} \int{\color{red}{3x^2}}\sin{x^3}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}x}&=\int{\sin{u}}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}\\ &=\int{\sin{u}}\ \mathrm{d}u\\ &=-\cos{u}+C\\ &=-\cos{x^3}+C \end{align*}‚ąę3x2sinx3 dx‚Äč=‚ąęsinu du=‚ąęsinu du=‚ąícosu+C=‚ąícosx3+C‚Äč Hence: ‚ąę3x2sin‚Ā°x3 dx=‚ąícos‚Ā°x3+C‚Ė†\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=-\cos{x^3}+C \tag*{$\blacksquare$}‚ąę3x2sinx3 dx=‚ąícosx3+C‚Ė† By replacing the variable xxx into a function of uuu, we can simplify the overall question to a common integral. As you can see, this is a method we inversely use the Chain Rule, and generally, it is called: The substitution rule Given a differenciable function u=g(x)u=g(x)u=g(x), and fff is continuous on its interval, then: ‚ąęf(g(x))g‚Ä≤(x) dx=‚ąęf(u) du\int{f\left(g\left(x\right)\right)g'(x)}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f\left(u\right)}\ \mathrm{d}u‚ąęf(g(x))g‚Ä≤(x) dx=‚ąęf(u) du Definite Integral In the case of Definite Integral, the bound should also changes, as: ‚ąęabf(g(x))g‚Ä≤(x) dx=‚ąęg(a)g(b)f(u) du\int_{a}^{b}{f\left(g\left(x\right)\right)g'(x)}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{g(a)}^{g(b)}{f\left(u\right)}\ \mathrm{d}u‚ąęab‚Äčf(g(x))g‚Ä≤(x) dx=‚ąęg(a)g(b)‚Äčf(u) du

Direct Substitution is a method created by the author himself. It simplify the process of substitution without write out uuu and du\mathrm{d}udu. I will show this with an example: ‚ąę3x2sin‚Ā°x3 dx= ?\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\ ?‚ąę3x2sinx3 dx= ? This time, let's try what I called Direct Substituton: Direct Substitution Approach After analyze, we want to substitute x3x^3x3. All we need to do is substitude dx\mathrm{d}xdx to a fraction where the top is dx3\mathrm{d}x^3dx3 and the bottom is the derivative of x3x^3x3 based on xxx, as: ‚ąę3x2sin‚Ā°x3 dx=‚ąę3x2sin‚Ā°x3 d(x3)3x2=‚ąęsin‚Ā°x3 d(x3)\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{\cancel{3x^2}\sin{x^3}}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(x^3)}{\cancel{3x^2}}=\int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)‚ąę3x2sinx3 dx=‚ąę3x2sinx3 3x2d(x3)‚Äč=‚ąęsinx3 d(x3) And all we need is to consider x3x^3x3 as uuu and integrate normally: ‚üĻ ‚ąęsin‚Ā°x3 d(x3)=‚ąícos‚Ā°x3+C\implies \int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}+C‚üĻ‚ąęsinx3 d(x3)=‚ąícosx3+C More importantly, for definite integral, it doesn't need to change bounds, as: ‚ąęab3x2sin‚Ā°x3 dx=‚ąęabsin‚Ā°x3 d(x3)=‚ąícos‚Ā°x3‚ą£ab=‚ąícos‚Ā°b3+cos‚Ā°a3\int_{a}^{b}3x^2\sin{x^3}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{a}^{b}{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}\big|_{a}^{b}=-\cos{b^3}+\cos{a^3}‚ąęab‚Äč3x2sinx3 dx=‚ąęab‚Äčsinx3 d(x3)=‚ąícosx3‚Äčab‚Äč=‚ąícosb3+cosa3 Direct Substitution In general, if we want to substitude ax+bax+bax+b or term that can cancel the other one, we may use direct substitution ‚ąęf(x)g(x)‚ŹüSubstitude dx=‚ąęf(x)g(x) d(g(x))g‚Ä≤(x)\int{{f(x)}\underbrace{g(x)}_{\text{Substitude}}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f(x)g(x)}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(g(x))}{g'(x)} ‚ąęf(x)Substitudeg(x)‚Äč‚Äč dx=‚ąęf(x)g(x) g‚Ä≤(x)d(g(x))‚Äč

sin‚Ā°(őĪ+ő≤)=sin‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤+cos‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤\sin{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\sin{\alpha}\cos{\beta}+\cos{\alpha}\sin{\beta}sin(őĪ+ő≤)=sinőĪcoső≤+cosőĪsinő≤ Derivation sin‚Ā°ő≤=BCAB=BC1 ‚üĻ BC=sin‚Ā°ő≤cos‚Ā°ő≤=ACAB=AC1 ‚üĻ AC=cos‚Ā°ő≤sin‚Ā°őĪ=CDAC=CDcos‚Ā°ő≤ ‚üĻ CD=sin‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤cos‚Ā°őĪ=CFBC=CFsin‚Ā°ő≤ ‚üĻ CF=cos‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AC}=\frac{CD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies CD&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BC}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinő≤=ABBC‚Äč=1BC‚Äč‚üĻBCcoső≤=ABAC‚Äč=1AC‚Äč‚üĻACsinőĪ=ACCD‚Äč=coső≤CD‚Äč‚üĻCDcosőĪ=BCCF‚Äč=sinő≤CF‚Äč‚üĻCF‚Äč=sinő≤=coső≤=sinőĪcoső≤=cosőĪsinő≤‚Äčsin‚Ā°(őĪ+ő≤)=BEAB=BE1=DF=CD+CF=sin‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤+cos‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤ \begin{align*} \sin{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BE}{AB}=\frac{BE}{1}=DF\\ &=CD+CF\\ &=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}+\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin(őĪ+ő≤)‚Äč=ABBE‚Äč=1BE‚Äč=DF=CD+CF=sinőĪcoső≤+cosőĪsinő≤‚Äč‚Ė†‚Äč

cos‚Ā°(őĪ+ő≤)=cos‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤‚ąísin‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤\cos{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\cos{\alpha}\cos{\beta}-\sin{\alpha}\sin{\beta}cos(őĪ+ő≤)=cosőĪcoső≤‚ąísinőĪsinő≤ Derivation sin‚Ā°ő≤=BCAB=BC1 ‚üĻ BC=sin‚Ā°ő≤cos‚Ā°ő≤=ACAB=AC1 ‚üĻ AC=cos‚Ā°ő≤sin‚Ā°őĪ=BFAC=BFsin‚Ā°ő≤ ‚üĻ BF=sin‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤cos‚Ā°őĪ=ADBC=ADcos‚Ā°ő≤ ‚üĻ AD=cos‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{AC}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{BC}=\frac{AD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies AD&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinő≤=ABBC‚Äč=1BC‚Äč‚üĻBCcoső≤=ABAC‚Äč=1AC‚Äč‚üĻACsinőĪ=ACBF‚Äč=sinő≤BF‚Äč‚üĻBFcosőĪ=BCAD‚Äč=coső≤AD‚Äč‚üĻAD‚Äč=sinő≤=coső≤=sinőĪsinő≤=cosőĪcoső≤‚Äčcos‚Ā°(őĪ+ő≤)=AEAB=AE1=AD‚ąíED=AD‚ąíBF=cos‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤‚ąísin‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤ \begin{align*} \cos{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{AE}{AB}=\frac{AE}{1}\\ &=AD-ED\\ &=AD-BF\\ &=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}-\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos(őĪ+ő≤)‚Äč=ABAE‚Äč=1AE‚Äč=AD‚ąíED=AD‚ąíBF=cosőĪcoső≤‚ąísinőĪsinő≤‚Äč‚Ė†‚Äč

cos‚Ā°(őĪ‚ąíő≤)=cos‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤+sin‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤\cos{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\cos{\alpha}\cos{\beta}+\sin{\alpha}\sin{\beta}cos(őĪ‚ąíő≤)=cosőĪcoső≤+sinőĪsinő≤ Derivation sin‚Ā°ő≤=BCAB=BC1 ‚üĻ BC=sin‚Ā°ő≤cos‚Ā°ő≤=ACAB=AC1 ‚üĻ AC=cos‚Ā°ő≤sin‚Ā°őĪ=CFBC=CFsin‚Ā°ő≤ ‚üĻ CF=sin‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤cos‚Ā°őĪ=CDAC=CDcos‚Ā°ő≤ ‚üĻ CD=cos‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BC}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AC}=\frac{CD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies CD&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinő≤=ABBC‚Äč=1BC‚Äč‚üĻBCcoső≤=ABAC‚Äč=1AC‚Äč‚üĻACsinőĪ=BCCF‚Äč=sinő≤CF‚Äč‚üĻCFcosőĪ=ACCD‚Äč=coső≤CD‚Äč‚üĻCD‚Äč=sinő≤=coső≤=sinőĪsinő≤=cosőĪcoső≤‚Äčcos‚Ā°(őĪ‚ąíő≤)=AGAB=AG1=DF=CD+CF=cos‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°ő≤+sin‚Ā°őĪsin‚Ā°ő≤ \begin{align*} \cos{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{AG}{AB}=\frac{AG}{1}=DF\\ &=CD+CF\\ &=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}+\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos(őĪ‚ąíő≤)‚Äč=ABAG‚Äč=1AG‚Äč=DF=CD+CF=cosőĪcoső≤+sinőĪsinő≤‚Äč‚Ė†‚Äč

tan‚Ā°(őĪ‚ąíő≤)=tan‚Ā°őĪ‚ąítan‚Ā°ő≤1+tan‚Ā°őĪtan‚Ā°ő≤\tan{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\frac{\tan{\alpha}-\tan{\beta}}{1+\tan{\alpha}\tan{\beta}}tan(őĪ‚ąíő≤)=1+tanőĪtanő≤tanőĪ‚ąítanő≤‚Äč Derivation tan‚Ā°őĪ=ADCD=AD1 ‚üĻ AD=tan‚Ā°őĪsec‚Ā°őĪ=ACCD=AC1 ‚üĻ AC=sec‚Ā°őĪtan‚Ā°ő≤=BCAC=BCsec‚Ā°őĪ ‚üĻ BC=sec‚Ā°őĪtan‚Ā°ő≤sec‚Ā°őĪ=BCBF=sec‚Ā°őĪtan‚Ā°ő≤BF ‚üĻ BF=tan‚Ā°ő≤tan‚Ā°őĪ=CFBF=CFtan‚Ā°ő≤ ‚üĻ CF=tan‚Ā°őĪtan‚Ā°ő≤ \begin{align*} \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{CD}=\frac{AD}{1}\implies AD&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{AC}{CD}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\\ \blue{\tan{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AC}=\frac{BC}{\green{\sec{\alpha}}}\implies BC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{BC}{BF}=\frac{\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{BF}\implies BF&=\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BF}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\tan{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}} \end{align*}tanőĪ=CDAD‚Äč=1AD‚Äč‚üĻADsecőĪ=CDAC‚Äč=1AC‚Äč‚üĻACtanő≤=ACBC‚Äč=secőĪBC‚Äč‚üĻBCsecőĪ=BFBC‚Äč=BFsecőĪtanő≤‚Äč‚üĻBFtanőĪ=BFCF‚Äč=tanő≤CF‚Äč‚üĻCF‚Äč=tanőĪ=secőĪ=secőĪtanő≤=tanő≤=tanőĪtanő≤‚Äčtan‚Ā°(őĪ‚ąíő≤)=BGAG=AEDF=AD‚ąíBFCD+CF=tan‚Ā°őĪ‚ąítan‚Ā°ő≤1+tan‚Ā°őĪtan‚Ā°ő≤ \begin{align*} \tan{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BG}{AG}=\frac{AE}{DF}=\frac{AD-BF}{CD+CF}\\ &=\frac{\green{\tan{\alpha}}-\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{1+\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}tan(őĪ‚ąíő≤)‚Äč=AGBG‚Äč=DFAE‚Äč=CD+CFAD‚ąíBF‚Äč=1+tanőĪtanő≤tanőĪ‚ąítanő≤‚Äč‚Äč‚Ė†‚Äč

sin‚Ā°3őĪ=‚ąí4sin‚Ā°3őĪ+3sin‚Ā°őĪ\sin{3\alpha}=-4\sin^3{\alpha}+3\sin{\alpha}sin3őĪ=‚ąí4sin3őĪ+3sinőĪ Derivation sin‚Ā°3őĪ=sin‚Ā°(2őĪ+őĪ)=sin‚Ā°2őĪcos‚Ā°őĪ+cos‚Ā°2őĪsin‚Ā°őĪ(Trig Identity #4)=2sin‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°őĪcos‚Ā°őĪ+cos‚Ā°2őĪsin‚Ā°őĪ(Trig Identity #10)=2sin‚Ā°őĪ(cos‚Ā°2őĪ)+(1‚ąí2sin‚Ā°2őĪ)sin‚Ā°őĪ(Trig Identity #11)=2sin‚Ā°őĪ(1‚ąísin‚Ā°2őĪ)+(1‚ąí2sin‚Ā°2őĪ)sin‚Ā°őĪ(Trig Identity #1)=2sin‚Ā°őĪ‚ąí2sin‚Ā°3őĪ+sin‚Ā°őĪ‚ąí2sin‚Ā°3őĪ=‚ąí4sin‚Ā°3őĪ+3sin‚Ā°őĪ\begin{align*} \sin{3\alpha}&=\sin{({2\alpha}+{\alpha})}\\ &=\sin{{2\alpha}}\cos{{\alpha}}+\cos{{2\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#4}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}\cos{\alpha}\cos{\alpha}+\cos{{2\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#10}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}(\cos^2{\alpha})+(1-2\sin^2{\alpha})\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#11}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}(1-\sin^2{\alpha})+(1-2\sin^2{\alpha})\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#1}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}-2\sin^3{\alpha}+\sin{\alpha}-2\sin^3{\alpha}\\ &=-4\sin^3{\alpha}+3\sin{\alpha} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin3őĪ‚Äč=sin(2őĪ+őĪ)=sin2őĪcosőĪ+cos2őĪsinőĪ=2sinőĪcosőĪcosőĪ+cos2őĪsinőĪ=2sinőĪ(cos2őĪ)+(1‚ąí2sin2őĪ)sinőĪ=2sinőĪ(1‚ąísin2őĪ)+(1‚ąí2sin2őĪ)sinőĪ=2sinőĪ‚ąí2sin3őĪ+sinőĪ‚ąí2sin3őĪ=‚ąí4sin3őĪ+3sinőĪ‚Äč‚Äč(Trig Identity #4)(Trig Identity #10)(Trig Identity #11)(Trig Identity #1)‚Äč‚Ė†‚Äč Trig Identity #4 \quad Trig Identity #10 Trig Identity #11 \quad Trig Identity #1

Let's discover Docusaurus in less than 5 minutes.
Get started by creating a new site. Or try Docusaurus immediately with docusaurus.new.
Node.js version 20.0 or above: When installing Node.js, you are recommended to check all checkboxes related to dependencies.

Generate a new Docusaurus site using the classic template. The classic template will automatically be added to your project after you run the command: npm init docusaurus@latest my-website classic You can type this command into Command Prompt, Powershell, Terminal, or any other integrated terminal of your code editor. The command also installs all necessary dependencies you need to run Docusaurus.

Run the development server: cd my-website npm run start The cd command changes the directory you're working with. In order to work with your newly created Docusaurus site, you'll need to navigate the terminal there. The npm run start command builds your website locally and serves it through a development server, ready for you to view at http://localhost:3000/. Open docs/intro.md (this page) and edit some lines: the site reloads automatically and displays your changes.
